category_title: Low-achieving vocational students in a computer maintenance class
minimal_context: The student is working on a PC in the classroom to design a laptop flyer using Photoshop.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please start working on your PCs to complete the design of a laptop flyer using Photoshop.

STUDENT: (Turns to a classmate) Can you help me understand the required assignment and how to implement it? These regular assignments make me get bored very quickly.

TUTOR: Try to figure it out together with your peer.

STUDENT: I perceive this task to be difficult to achieve. I don't know how to do the design.

TUTOR: Just keep trying your best on the flyer.

STUDENT: (Did not participate) I'm not doing this. I'm going to open YouTube and then play collectively on Counterstrike.

options.A: Divide the flyer assignment into small chunks and give the student each chunk one at a time.
options.B: Refer other students to him for advice when they face a Photoshop challenge he has successfully navigated.
options.C: Tell the student their work looks good and ask what color could pull the flyer design together.
options.D: Use a "request for clarification" to ask the student to explain their issue with the flyer design.
